title: About the Author
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: This biographical summary introduces Peter S. Kastner as a fifty-year participant in the information technology industry, tracing his path from a Cape Cod restaurant bookkeeping department through MIT, Cornell, and a succession of influential roles at Philip Hankins, Arthur D. Little, Prime Computer, Stratus Computer, Digital Equipment Corporation, and Aberdeen Group. It highlights his role in founding Aberdeen Group, building its research practice to 150 analysts, co-founding the Transaction Processing Performance Council, and maintaining an active expert witness practice in technology disputes.
importance_rationale: This section establishes Kastner's credentials and the arc of his career, providing essential context for all subsequent chapters of the memoir.
relevance_rationale: Every major entity, role, and theme referenced in the memoir is introduced here, making this the foundational reference for the entire volume.
prescience_rationale: This is a biographical summary rather than a forward-looking analysis, so prescience scoring does not apply.
